exercises.title: Bit-Flip Encoder
exercises.description: Implement the encoding circuit for the 3-qubit bit-flip code.
tasks: Create a function to encode arbitrary single-qubit states
Implement the encoding using matrix operations (CNOTs)
Verify encoding of |0⟩, |1⟩, |+⟩, and |-⟩
Check that encoded states are properly normalized
Verify entanglement by computing reduced density matrices
exercises.expectedOutput: Encoder with verification for standard states
hints: First CNOT: qubits 1,2. Second CNOT: qubits 1,3
Trace out qubits to get reduced density matrix

exercises.title: Syndrome Measurement Simulator
exercises.description: Implement syndrome measurement and error identification.
tasks: Build the stabilizer operators Z₁Z₂ and Z₂Z₃
Implement syndrome extraction from quantum states
Create syndrome lookup table for error correction
Test with all single-qubit X errors
Verify syndrome correctly identifies error locations
exercises.expectedOutput: Working syndrome decoder with test results
hints: Syndrome bit = 0 if eigenvalue +1, 1 if eigenvalue -1
Test on states like X₁|000⟩, X₂|000⟩, X₃|000⟩

exercises.title: Error Correction Simulator
exercises.description: Implement the complete error correction cycle.
tasks: Combine encoding, error injection, syndrome, and correction
Simulate random single X errors with probability p
Measure logical error rate after correction
Compare with theoretical rate (3p² for small p)
Plot error rate vs physical error probability
hints: Logical error occurs when 2+ X errors happen
Run many trials for accurate statistics
Break-even point is where p_logical < p_physical

exercises.title: Phase Error Vulnerability
exercises.description: Demonstrate and analyze the code's weakness to Z errors.
tasks: Apply Z errors and show syndrome remains (0,0)
Calculate the logical error caused by each Z_i
Show that Z₁Z₂Z₃ is a logical Z operator
Compute fidelity loss from undetected Z errors
Design a metric for 'Z error sensitivity'
exercises.expectedOutput: Analysis of Z error vulnerability with visualizations
hints: All single Z errors cause logical Z
Z_L = Z₁ = Z₂ = Z₃ = Z₁Z₂Z₃ (all equivalent)
